project.name: course-dev-ide-with-agents
project.description: Collaborative intelligence infrastructure for course content production - multi-agent orchestrator (Marcus) + per-specialist LangGraph 9-node scaffolds + HIL gates with tamper-evident operator verdicts, completing full production runs from corpus to composition hand-off.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: App-namespace Marcus compatibility surface that re-exports get_facade as the package's public entry.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: CLI package barrel re-exporting the ad-hoc ask, gate verdict/override, and trial-launch command entry points.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: python -m app.marcus.cli entry point that assembles the top-level argparse parser with trial, adhoc, and gate subcommands and dispatches to the matching CLI handler.

nodes.name: adhoc_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI bridge for one-shot Marcus ad-hoc asks: parses cascade overrides, loads env, calls the Marcus facade's ask, and prints the structured AdhocResponse.

nodes.name: gate_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI bridge for the HIL gate verdict and override flow: validates OperatorVerdict payloads, resumes runs from verdicts, and submits/applies cache-impact overrides.

nodes.name: _shim_parser.py
nodes.summary: Shared argparse factory for the A2 single-decision gate shims; single source of truth for the shim parser shape and the audience-layered OPERATOR/INPUTS/OUTPUTS/REFERENCE help text pinned by AC-7.7-B.
nodes.languageNotes: Help-text section headers and underline lengths are exact-match pinned for grep stability across the four per-gate shims.

nodes.name: g1_shim.py
nodes.summary: A2 single-decision CLI shim for terminal gate G1: validates an OperatorVerdict file and resumes the paused production trial at that gate.

nodes.name: g2c_shim.py
nodes.summary: A2 single-decision CLI shim for terminal gate G2C: validates an OperatorVerdict file and resumes the paused production trial at that gate.

nodes.name: g3_shim.py
nodes.summary: A2 single-decision CLI shim for terminal gate G3: validates an OperatorVerdict file and resumes the paused production trial at that gate.

nodes.name: g4_shim.py
nodes.summary: A2 single-decision CLI shim for terminal gate G4: validates an OperatorVerdict file and resumes the paused production trial at that gate.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Assembles the top-level argparse parser with trial, adhoc, and gate subcommand groups.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses argv and routes to the trial, adhoc, or gate CLI handler, returning a process exit code.

nodes.name: run_adhoc_ask
nodes.summary: Executes one Marcus ad-hoc ask via the facade and returns the structured response payload.

nodes.name: adhoc_ask_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse handler for the adhoc subcommand: loads env, applies cascade overrides, runs the ask, and prints JSON or text output with error handling.

nodes.name: build_adhoc_parser
nodes.summary: Registers the adhoc subcommand's arguments on a provided argparse parser.

nodes.name: run_gate_decide
nodes.summary: Validates an OperatorVerdict payload, resumes the run from the verdict, and wraps the resume command in a CLI transport response.

nodes.name: run_override_submit
nodes.summary: Submits a cache-impact override request for a trial and returns the pending override payload.

nodes.name: run_override_apply
nodes.summary: Applies a previously submitted override using its confirm token and returns the applied-override response.

nodes.name: gate_decide_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse handler for the gate decide subcommand: reads the verdict JSON file and prints the resume transport response.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the gate CLI parser with decide, override-submit, and override-apply subcommands.

nodes.name: build_shim_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the per-gate shim argparse parser from the shared template, embedding the gate id in the pinned four-section help text.

nodes.name: shim_main
nodes.summary: Validates the operator verdict file for gate G1 and resumes the production trial, mapping validation and gate errors to distinct exit codes.

nodes.name: shim_main
nodes.summary: Validates the operator verdict file for gate G2C and resumes the production trial, mapping validation and gate errors to distinct exit codes.

nodes.name: shim_main
nodes.summary: Validates the operator verdict file for gate G3 and resumes the production trial, mapping validation and gate errors to distinct exit codes.

nodes.name: shim_main
nodes.summary: Validates the operator verdict file for gate G4 and resumes the production trial, mapping validation and gate errors to distinct exit codes.

nodes.name: trial.py
nodes.summary: CLI shim for production-clone trial launches: handles G0 directive confirmation/editing in the operator's editor, starts, resumes, and recovers production trials, and writes cancellation records.

nodes.name: facade.py
nodes.summary: Single Maya-facing Marcus facade (Story 30-1 duality split) that hides internal sub-package boundaries; exposes run_step, ask, and run_4a behind one consolidated 'Marcus' voice with a binding first-person voice register.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level singleton accessed via get_facade()/reset_facade(); Maya-visible strings must use first-person present tense per R1 amendment 17.

nodes.name: conversation_persistence.py
nodes.summary: Persists per-gate conversation turns with a tamper-evident SHA256 digest chain, supporting ordered turn loading, latest-digest lookup, and full chain verification.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses canonical JSON byte serialization (sorted keys, compact separators) so digests are stable across processes.

nodes.name: dispatch_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Production composition adapter that builds specialist state from the manifest, compiles LangGraph specialist graphs with an in-memory checkpointer, invokes them, and folds outputs back into the ProductionEnvelope as contributions.

nodes.name: gate_runner.py
nodes.summary: Shared gate-runner guardrails: calibration tripwire over operator override history, engagement-decay reporting, and Marcus duality-boundary assertions that keep orchestration and operator-handoff state separated.

nodes.name: package_builders.py
nodes.summary: S3 pre-dispatch package builders: pure deterministic (no-LLM) transforms that project a ratified lesson plan plus creative directive into Gary slide-brief payloads keyed to the manifest's pre-dispatch build node.

nodes.name: pre_gate_marcus.py
nodes.summary: Pre-gate-marcus shared LLM node (Story 7a.3): renders a Jinja2 template-with-slots prompt per conversational gate (G1/G2C/G3/G4) and parses the model reply into a structured PreFillProposal for decision-card drafting.
nodes.languageNotes: Jinja2 Environment uses StrictUndefined so missing slot values fail loudly instead of rendering blanks.

nodes.name: production_runner.py
nodes.summary: Production-graph runner composition layer: walks the compiled manifest graph node by node, dispatches specialists, pauses at HIL gates with decision cards (optionally pre-filled by pre-gate-marcus), checkpoints state, records cost/trace evidence, and supports resume and recover flows.
nodes.languageNotes: Single 1900-line composition module deliberately concentrating the trial walk, pause/resume/recover, checkpointing, LangSmith tracing, and economics recording seams.

nodes.name: specialist_summary_writer.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator-facing barrel for the specialist summary writer API; the implementation lives in app.models.state so specialist graphs can emit summaries without importing app.marcus and breaking lane contracts.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Runtime-owned Pydantic model barrel re-exporting AdhocResponse, ProductionEnvelope, ProductionTrialEnvelope, and the trial-economics report family.

nodes.name: _confirm_or_edit_directive
nodes.summary: Interactive G0 loop that shows the composed directive and lets the operator confirm, edit in $EDITOR, or decline before specialists are dispatched.

nodes.name: start_trial
nodes.summary: Starts a production-clone trial: composes the Section 02A directive from corpus input, runs G0 confirmation, sets up the runs directory, and launches run_production_trial with economics and gate settings.

nodes.name: start_trial_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse handler that maps trial start CLI flags onto start_trial and prints the trial outcome.

nodes.name: resume_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused trial from an operator verdict file and reports the resulting trial state.

nodes.name: recover_trial
nodes.summary: Recovers an error-paused trial from its last checkpoint and reports the resulting trial state.

nodes.name: build_trial_parser
nodes.summary: Registers start, resume, and recover trial subcommands with their full flag sets on a provided parser.

nodes.name: Facade
nodes.summary: The single Maya-facing Marcus surface: exposes marcus_identity, run_step, ask (one-shot LLM with economics), and run_4a workflow routing while keeping internal routing identifiers invisible.

nodes.name: get_facade
nodes.summary: Returns the lazily constructed module-level Facade singleton, wiring sanctum digest, activation allowlist, and cascade config.

nodes.name: compute_turn_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA256 chain digest for a conversation turn from the prior envelope digest, decision card, timestamp, and operator id.

nodes.name: write_turn
nodes.summary: Persists a gate conversation turn JSON with its chained digest, schema version, and turn index under the trial's conversation directory.

nodes.name: verify_chain
nodes.summary: Re-walks all persisted turns for a trial and verifies the SHA256 digest chain, raising ConversationChainBrokenError on any mismatch.

nodes.name: ProductionDispatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Builds specialist dispatch state from manifest nodes and envelope dependencies, compiles the specialist's LangGraph builder with an InMemorySaver checkpointer, invokes it, and extracts the output contribution with the model used.

nodes.name: CalibrationTripwireConfig
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding calibration tripwire thresholds: window size, override-rate threshold, disagreement-axis threshold, and auto-lock trial count.

nodes.name: evaluate_calibration_tripwire
nodes.summary: Evaluates operator override history against tripwire thresholds at a gate and appends a JSONL tripwire log entry, signalling when calibration review or auto-lock should fire.

nodes.name: emit_engagement_decay_report
nodes.summary: Compares first-quartile vs last-quartile engagement event counts for a trial and writes a markdown engagement-decay report when the ratio drops below threshold.

nodes.name: assert_marcus_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Raises MarcusDualityBoundaryError when orchestrator-mode state and operator-handoff state keys overlap.

nodes.name: assert_payload_duality_boundary
nodes.summary: Asserts a specialist payload carries no operator-handoff keys before dispatch, enforcing the Marcus duality boundary at the payload level.

nodes.name: BuilderInputError
nodes.summary: Error raised when a pre-dispatch builder's upstream envelope inputs are missing or malformed, carrying the offending node and key context.

nodes.name: build_gary_briefs
nodes.summary: Deterministically projects a ratified lesson plan plus creative directive into per-slide Gary brief payloads restricted to Gary's consumed-payload vocabulary.

nodes.name: run_builder_node
nodes.summary: Resolves and runs the registered builder for a manifest node against the production envelope, returning the built payload as a node-keyed contribution output.

nodes.name: PreFillProposal
nodes.summary: Structured pre-fill proposal for a gate decision card: decision, directive, rationale, confidence, and confidence signals.

nodes.name: render_pre_fill_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the per-gate Jinja2 template with slot values into the pre-gate-marcus prompt text.

nodes.name: invoke_pre_gate_marcus
nodes.summary: Makes the single pre-gate LLM call: renders the prompt, invokes the marcus chat model, and parses the reply into a PreFillProposal.

nodes.name: MissingUpstreamContributionError
nodes.summary: Raised when a downstream specialist's dependency map references upstream contributions absent from the production envelope, naming the missing specialists.

nodes.name: LegacyEnvelopeSchemaError
nodes.summary: Raised when a persisted envelope on disk uses a legacy schema version that the current runner cannot safely resume.

nodes.name: _build_decision_card
nodes.summary: Builds the gate-specific decision card for a pause point, merging card metadata, pending nodes, artifact paths, and any pre-gate-marcus drafted proposal.

nodes.name: _write_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Writes the trial checkpoint JSON (node index, gate, run state, envelope, manifest binding, limits) enabling resume and recover flows.

nodes.name: _invoke_pre_gate_marcus_for_gate
nodes.summary: Conditionally invokes the pre-gate-marcus LLM node for a gate and returns the PreFillProposal plus its trace run for cost attribution.

nodes.name: _pause_at_gate
nodes.summary: Pauses the trial at a HIL gate: builds and registers the decision card, persists the conversation turn, publishes storyboard artifacts, emits the run summary, records cost, and writes the checkpoint.

nodes.name: _dispatch_specialist_at_node
nodes.summary: Dispatches one specialist at a manifest node through the ProductionDispatchAdapter, recording the contribution, trace run, and specialist summary.

nodes.name: _pause_at_error
nodes.summary: Error-pause seam: persists an error decision card and checkpoint when a specialist dispatch fails so the operator can recover or abort the trial.

nodes.name: run_production_trial
nodes.summary: Top-level trial entry: loads and compiles the pipeline manifest, initializes run state and envelopes, then walks the production graph dispatching specialists and pausing at gates until terminal or paused.

nodes.name: resume_production_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a gate-paused trial from an operator verdict: re-registers the decision card from disk, applies the verdict to run state, and continues the graph walk from the checkpoint.

nodes.name: recover_production_trial
nodes.summary: Recovers an error-paused trial from its last checkpoint without an operator verdict, resuming the walk at the failed node.

nodes.name: _continue_production_walk
nodes.summary: Shared graph-walk loop used by start, resume, and recover: iterates manifest nodes from a start index, runs builder/orchestration/specialist nodes, enforces specialist-call budgets, and pauses at gates or errors.

nodes.name: adhoc_response.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic response contract for one-shot Marcus ad-hoc asks, pairing the answer text with token usage and inline cost metadata.

nodes.name: production_envelope.py
nodes.summary: Strict cross-specialist accumulator for production composition: SpecialistContribution entries with SHA-256 output digests collected into a per-trial ProductionEnvelope with node-keyed lookup.

nodes.name: production_trial_envelope.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic envelope for production-graph trial lifecycle state (registered, in-flight, paused-at-gate, completed...) with timezone and UUID4 enforcement and lifecycle invariants.

nodes.name: trial_economics_report.py
nodes.summary: Strict per-trial economics report models: per-agent and per-model cost breakdowns, drift alerts against rolling medians, and budget status, all digest-bound to the cascade and pricing configs.

nodes.name: operator_verdict.py
nodes.summary: OperatorVerdict frozen value object for HIL gate decisions (FR31/FR34): approve/edit/reject verbs bound to a decision-card digest, with a small CLI to emit its JSON Schema.
nodes.languageNotes: Tamper verbs (timeout, auto_approve) are rejected by a triple-layer defense: Literal type, validator re-check, and schema pin.

nodes.name: specialist_summary_artifacts.py
nodes.summary: Specialist summary persistence for adjacent gate context: builds length-validated summary text from specialist state, emits it under the trial's runs directory, and loads the most recent adjacent summary.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_validators.py
nodes.summary: Standalone cross-field validators for OperatorVerdict: rejects tamper verbs (second layer of the FR34 triple-layer defense) and enforces verb/payload consistency for edit and reject verdicts.

nodes.name: cascade_config.py
nodes.summary: Operator-editable model-cascade and pricing config loaders: parses runtime YAML configs into digest-stamped Pydantic models, resolves per-agent model assignments with alias collision checks, and verifies pricing coverage.

nodes.name: economics.py
nodes.summary: Per-trial cost measurement and persistence for the migrated runtime: flattens LangSmith traces, attributes billable LLM runs to agents/models, computes drift against report history and budget status, and renders/records markdown cost reports.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Gary consumed-payload contract (S1, segment-data-plane): declares the frozen set of envelope payload keys Gary's act body reads, locking manifest dependency edges and the S3 package builder to one shared vocabulary.

nodes.name: TokenCount
nodes.summary: Strict token-usage model validating that total_tokens covers input plus output tokens.

nodes.name: AdhocResponse
nodes.summary: Marcus ad-hoc response contract bundling answer text, model used, token counts, and cost in USD.

nodes.name: compute_output_digest
nodes.summary: Returns a stable SHA-256 digest of a specialist output payload using canonical JSON serialization.

nodes.name: SpecialistContribution
nodes.summary: One specialist's envelope contribution: output payload with enforced SHA-256 digest, node id, attempt counter, cost, and model used, constructed via from_output.

nodes.name: ProductionEnvelope
nodes.summary: Per-trial accumulator of specialist contributions with UUID4-enforced trial id, node-keyed get_contribution lookup, latest_for_specialist, and append-style add_contribution.

nodes.name: ProductionTrialEnvelope
nodes.summary: Trial lifecycle envelope enforcing UUID4 trial id, timezone-aware start/completion timestamps, and status-transition invariants across the production trial lifecycle.

nodes.name: AgentCostEntry
nodes.summary: Per-agent aggregate cost attribution: assigned model, call count, token totals, and cost in USD.

nodes.name: DriftAlert
nodes.summary: Drift alert comparing an agent's observed per-call cost against its rolling median with deviation percentage.

nodes.name: BudgetStatus
nodes.summary: Budget status snapshot recording the budget state and any over-budget amount in USD.

nodes.name: TrialEconomicsReport
nodes.summary: Top-level per-trial economics report: total cost, per-agent and per-model breakdowns, drift status, budget status, and digests binding it to the cascade and pricing configs.

nodes.name: OperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Frozen HIL verdict value object: approve/edit/reject verb bound to trial, gate, card id, operator, timestamp, and decision-card SHA-256 digest, with cross-field tamper and consistency invariants.

nodes.name: canonical_specialist_id
nodes.summary: Normalizes specialist id variants through SPECIALIST_ALIASES to the canonical specialist identifier.

nodes.name: build_summary_text
nodes.summary: Builds the fixed-format, length-validated specialist summary text covering received keys, decision, artifact paths, and resolution trail.

nodes.name: emit_summary
nodes.summary: Writes a specialist summary file under the trial's summary directory with a timestamped, gate-scoped filename.

nodes.name: emit_summary_for_state
nodes.summary: Convenience emitter that derives received keys, decision, artifacts, and trail from a specialist graph state and emits the summary.

nodes.name: load_most_recent_summary
nodes.summary: Loads the most recent specialist summary for a trial (optionally before a cutoff) as an AdjacentSummary for adjacent gate context.

nodes.name: enforce_no_tamper_verbs
nodes.summary: Rejects tamper verbs (timeout, auto_approve) as the second layer of the FR34 triple-layer red-rejection, covering validate_assignment mutations and model_construct paths.

nodes.name: enforce_verb_payload_consistency
nodes.summary: Enforces that edit verdicts carry edit_payload, reject verdicts carry reject_reason, and approve verdicts carry neither.

nodes.name: normalize_agent_name
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes agent identifiers across hyphen/underscore variants for cascade and pricing lookups.

nodes.name: CascadeConfig
nodes.summary: Digest-stamped model-cascade config mapping Marcus and each specialist to a model entry, with alias-collision rejection and alias-aware resolve_entry lookup.

nodes.name: PricingTable
nodes.summary: Digest-stamped per-model pricing table with compute_cost converting input/output token counts to USD.

nodes.name: load_cascade
nodes.summary: Loads the model cascade YAML from disk into a CascadeConfig with its SHA-256 digest attached.

nodes.name: load_pricing
nodes.summary: Loads the OpenAI pricing YAML from disk into a PricingTable with its SHA-256 digest attached.

nodes.name: ensure_pricing_covers_cascade
nodes.summary: Verifies every model referenced by the cascade has a pricing entry, raising on any gap.

nodes.name: check_trial_budget
nodes.summary: Compares a running cost total against the trial budget and returns the resulting BudgetStatus.

nodes.name: compute_per_agent_drift
nodes.summary: Computes per-agent cost drift for a report against rolling medians from historical reports, producing drift alerts above the deviation threshold.

nodes.name: measure_trial_cost
nodes.summary: Core cost measurement: gathers the trial's LangSmith trace (live client, supplied root, or fixture), attributes billable LLM runs to agents and models via cascade/pricing, and assembles the TrialEconomicsReport with drift and budget status.

nodes.name: record_trial_cost_report
nodes.summary: Persists a trial's economics report as JSON plus rendered markdown under the trial's runs directory.

nodes.name: summarize_cost_reports
nodes.summary: Aggregates recent persisted cost reports under the runs root into a cross-trial summary.

nodes.name: load_trace_fixture
nodes.summary: Loads a recorded LangSmith trace fixture from JSON into the SimpleNamespace run tree used by measure_trial_cost.

nodes.name: resume_api.py
nodes.summary: Gate verdict resume substrate: registers decision cards with canonical-JSON SHA-256 digests at gate pause and converts operator verdicts into LangGraph Command resume payloads or transport responses.

nodes.name: StoredDecisionCard
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass pairing a registered decision card with its issuance timestamp, server nonce, and digest.

nodes.name: compute_decision_card_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the canonical-JSON SHA-256 digest binding a decision card to its trial, issuance timestamp, and server nonce.

nodes.name: register_decision_card
nodes.summary: Registers an issued decision card in the resume registry with a fresh server nonce and computed digest, enforcing tz-aware timestamps.

nodes.name: _resume_payload
nodes.summary: Builds the resume payload dict from an operator verdict for injection into the LangGraph Command.

nodes.name: build_transport_response
nodes.summary: Wraps a LangGraph resume Command and verdict into a transport-specific response shape for the requesting transport kind.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Validates an operator verdict against the registered decision card digest and produces the LangGraph Command that resumes the interrupted graph.

nodes.name: storyboard_publisher.py
nodes.summary: Publishes the ONLINE interactive storyboard at storyboard review gates by dynamically loading the legacy generate-storyboard.py script against the envelope's Gary contribution, before the decision card is issued; failures error-pause the trial recoverably.

nodes.name: StoryboardPublishError
nodes.summary: Typed dispatch error raised when storyboard publication fails; error-pauses the trial for recovery instead of killing the cycle.

nodes.name: _publisher_config
nodes.summary: Resolves publisher configuration (legacy script path and publish settings) from the environment and repo layout.

nodes.name: _load_generator_module
nodes.summary: Importlib-loads the legacy generate-storyboard.py script as a module so its generate/publish routines can be invoked in-process.

nodes.name: _write_segment_manifest_for_b
nodes.summary: Writes the segment manifest for Storyboard B from Irene's output so the generator can render the B incarnation.

nodes.name: publish_storyboard_for_gate
nodes.summary: Runs the full storyboard generate+publish routine at a storyboard review gate using the envelope's Gary contribution, returning the publish receipt before the decision card is issued.

nodes.name: adapter.py
nodes.summary: Thin ChatOpenAI factory that applies the three-level model-resolution cascade and returns an explicit (ChatOpenAI, ModelResolutionEntry) handle with LangSmith span metadata; deliberately avoids LCEL/agent abstractions per PRD Decision 3.
nodes.languageNotes: Explicit-return pattern chosen over ContextVar so the adapter stays pure-functional with respect to RunState.

nodes.name: ChatModelHandle
nodes.summary: NamedTuple pairing the constructed ChatOpenAI instance with its ModelResolutionEntry for the caller to append to the resolution trail.

nodes.name: _resolution_metadata
nodes.summary: Builds the LangSmith span-tag metadata dict (model_id, level, requested, resolved, reason, cache_prefix_hash) for a resolution entry.

nodes.name: make_chat_model
nodes.summary: Factory resolving the model cascade for a specialist and returning a plain ChatOpenAI plus its ModelResolutionEntry, with span metadata attached.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the nine Pydantic v2 state models that encode the LangGraph state contract and the five reproducibility invariants (NFR-X1 through X5).

nodes.name: cache_state.py
nodes.summary: CacheState Pydantic model tracking sanctum cold-read cache prefix, entry counts, and timezone-aware invalidation timestamps per architecture decision D5.

nodes.name: CacheState
nodes.summary: Pydantic model tracking cache prefix, entry count, and tz-aware last-invalidation timestamp for sanctum and model-resolution caches.

nodes.name: model_resolution_entry.py
nodes.summary: ModelResolutionEntry Pydantic model recording one level of the three-level model-resolution cascade, with a deterministic SHA-256 cache_prefix_hash anchoring the NFR-X4 audit trail and NFR-I6 cache-prefix stability.

nodes.name: ModelResolutionEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one cascade-resolution level (per_call/per_specialist/registry_default/auto_select_fallback) with tz-aware timestamp and SHA-256 hex cache_prefix_hash validators.

nodes.name: run_state.py
nodes.summary: RunState top-level Pydantic run-state model encoding the five reproducibility invariants: byte-for-byte replay, frozen graph version, sanctum fingerprint, model-resolution trail, and constrained temperature variance.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pydantic v2 field validators (UUID4, tz-aware datetimes, closed allow-lists) per the project's 14-idiom schema checklist.

nodes.name: RunState
nodes.summary: Top-level run-state Pydantic model with UUID4 run_id, frozen graph-version allow-list, sanctum/marcus fingerprints, model-resolution trail, story states, cache state, and production envelope, plus cross-field invariants.

nodes.name: run_state_validators.py
nodes.summary: Standalone cross-field validator enforcing that RunState.status='complete' if and only if completed_at is set, lifted out of the model so tests can exercise it without constructing a full RunState.

nodes.name: enforce_complete_requires_completed_at
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError unless status='complete' and completed_at are set together (and completed_at absent otherwise).

nodes.name: contract.py
nodes.summary: Canonical 9-node specialist scaffold contract and production-side authority: node-id set, conformance result dataclass, and specialist discovery/validation helpers used by every Slab 2+ specialist graph.

nodes.name: ScaffoldConformanceResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass capturing present/missing/extra node ids for a specialist graph with an is_conforming check.

nodes.name: HasNodes
nodes.summary: Structural Protocol for any compiled graph object exposing a nodes mapping.

nodes.name: discover_specialist_ids
nodes.summary: Discovers specialist ids by scanning the specialists root for packages shipping a graph.py.

nodes.name: build_specialist_graph
nodes.summary: Imports and invokes a specialist's build_<id>_graph factory by convention.

nodes.name: validate_scaffold
nodes.summary: Validates a compiled specialist subgraph against the canonical 9-node id set, returning a ScaffoldConformanceResult.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Canonical 9-node scaffold LangGraph StateGraph template (receive->plan->act->verify->reflect->emit_spans->gate_decision->finalize->handoff) with a transition-reachability assertion; the basis the specialist generator copies.

nodes.name: _assert_transition_reachability
nodes.summary: BFS over the declared transition tuples asserting every canonical scaffold node is reachable from receive.

nodes.name: build_scaffold_graph
nodes.summary: Builds and compiles the canonical 9-node scaffold StateGraph with interrupt-based gate_decision wiring.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Aria specialist 9-node scaffold graph: parses operator-instructions envelopes into build specs and invokes the LLM via make_chat_model in its act node.

nodes.name: OperatorInstructionsParseError
nodes.summary: Raised when the operator-instructions envelope payload cannot be parsed into a build spec.

nodes.name: _decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the specialist envelope payload JSON from run state.

nodes.name: _parse_operator_instructions_envelope
nodes.summary: Parses the operator-instructions payload into its structured fields, raising OperatorInstructionsParseError on malformed input.

nodes.name: _assemble_build_spec
nodes.summary: Assembles the parsed operator instructions into the build spec Aria's act node submits to the LLM.

nodes.name: _act
nodes.summary: Act node: decodes the envelope, assembles the build spec, invokes the chat model, and records the resolution trail entry.

nodes.name: build_aria_graph
nodes.summary: Builds and compiles Aria's 9-node scaffold-conformant StateGraph.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Creative-director (CD) specialist graph: sanctum-lock verification, reference-grounded prompt assembly, LLM dispatch, and strict JSON directive parsing with canonicalization and raw-excerpt error reporting.

nodes.name: CdDirectiveParseError
nodes.summary: Raised when the CD LLM response cannot be parsed or canonicalized into a directive; carries a raw-content excerpt for diagnosis.

nodes.name: _new_dispatch_trail_entry
nodes.summary: Creates a tagged ModelResolutionEntry continuing the dispatch trail from the last entry.

nodes.name: _read_sanctum_digest
nodes.summary: Reads and digests the CD sanctum directory in sorted POSIX-path order for prompt grounding and lock checks.

nodes.name: _current_sanctum_manifest
nodes.summary: Builds the current per-file hash manifest of the CD sanctum directory.

nodes.name: assert_sanctum_lock
nodes.summary: Asserts the sanctum manifest matches the expected snapshot, raising SanctumLockViolation on drift.

nodes.name: _read_cd_references
nodes.summary: Reads named CD reference documents from the references directory for prompt assembly.

nodes.name: _decode_envelope_payload
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the specialist envelope payload JSON from run state.

nodes.name: _profile_targets_prompt_block
nodes.summary: Renders the profile-targets block injected into the CD prompt.

nodes.name: _assemble_cd_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the CD directive-authoring prompt from envelope payload, extracted source, references, and profile targets.

nodes.name: _canonicalize_cd_directive
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes a parsed CD directive (key normalization, defaults, shape checks), raising CdDirectiveParseError with raw excerpt on violations.

nodes.name: _parse_cd_directive

nodes.name: base_client.py
nodes.summary: Base API client providing authenticated requests sessions, exponential-backoff retry, rate-limit handling, Link-header pagination, and structured error diagnostics that all tool-specific clients inherit.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a three-tier exception hierarchy (APIError, AuthenticationError, RateLimitError) and separates JSON-parsing requests (_request) from raw-bytes requests (_request_raw) so binary media endpoints reuse the same retry machinery.

nodes.name: APIError
nodes.summary: Base exception for API failures, carrying status code and response body for structured diagnostics.

nodes.name: AuthenticationError
nodes.summary: Raised on 401/403 responses indicating credential issues; subclass of APIError.

nodes.name: RateLimitError
nodes.summary: Raised on 429 responses after retry exhaustion; subclass of APIError.

nodes.name: BaseAPIClient
nodes.summary: Shared HTTP client base class wrapping a requests.Session with verb helpers (get/post/put/patch/delete), raw-bytes variants, Link-header pagination, Retry-After-aware backoff, and safe JSON parsing.

nodes.name: botpress_client.py

nodes.name: BotpressClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Botpress Cloud v1 API, resolving bot IDs from environment defaults and exposing conversation, intent-detection, and deployment operations.

nodes.name: canvas_client.py

nodes.name: CanvasClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Canvas LMS REST API with bearer-token auth and paginated list/create operations for courses, modules, pages, assignments, quizzes, and enrollments.

nodes.name: ElevenLabsClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the ElevenLabs API (xi-api-key auth) with 22 methods spanning TTS, timestamped TTS, pronunciation dictionaries, sound effects, dialogue, and music generation.

nodes.name: gamma_client.py

nodes.name: GammaClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Gamma developers API (X-API-KEY auth) with theme listing, template and prompt-based generation, and a wait_for_generation polling loop with timeout.

nodes.name: generate_jwt_token

nodes.name: KlingClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Kling AI API with auto-refreshing JWT auth, video generation endpoints (text-to-video, image-to-video, lip-sync, extend), status polling with backoff, and chunked video downloads.

nodes.name: _normalize_model_name
nodes.summary: Maps caller-supplied model name aliases to the canonical Kling model identifier appropriate for the requested mode.

nodes.name: _extract_duration
nodes.summary: Extracts the video duration field from variant Kling response payload shapes, tolerating missing or differently nested values.

nodes.name: _normalize_status_payload
nodes.summary: Normalizes heterogeneous task-status responses from the Singapore API surface into the common status/video-url/duration contract used by callers of the validated client.

nodes.name: KlingPublicClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass targeting Kling's newer public API surface, reusing generate_jwt_token from the validated client for auth while exposing text-to-video, image-to-video, status polling, and download.

nodes.name: notion_client.py

nodes.name: _block_to_lines

nodes.name: NotionClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Notion REST API with page retrieval, search, paginated block-children iteration, depth-first block walking, markdown export, and paragraph appending.

nodes.name: wondercraft_client.py

nodes.name: WondercraftClient
nodes.summary: BaseAPIClient subclass for the Wondercraft v1 API (X-API-KEY auth) exposing podcast creation variants, episode listing, and asynchronous job polling.

nodes.name: run_story33_kling_queue.py
nodes.summary: Operational script that runs Story 3.3 Kling validation clips as a serialized queue: submits one video job at a time with backoff, polls and downloads each result, and logs progress to a CSV manifest to respect Kling's concurrency limit.
nodes.languageNotes: Serialization is deliberate: Kling enforces a concurrency limit, so the safe path is submit, poll, download, and manifest-log before the next request.

nodes.name: write_manifest
nodes.summary: Writes the job queue state rows to the CSV manifest so progress survives interruptions and reruns.

nodes.name: submit_with_backoff
nodes.summary: Submits a single Kling video job, retrying with backoff when the API signals concurrency or rate-limit pressure, and returns the accepted task ID.

nodes.name: poll_and_download
nodes.summary: Polls a submitted Kling task until completion and downloads the resulting video to the named output file.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Queue driver: loads or initializes the manifest, iterates pending validation clips one at a time through submit, poll, and download, and persists manifest state after each job.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the v4.2 pack generator that re-exports the render entry point from render.py.

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Constructs the Jinja2 Environment for v4.2 pack rendering, wiring a FileSystemLoader rooted at the (overridable) templates directory.
nodes.languageNotes: The template_root parameter is an injection seam allowing tests to point the Jinja environment at alternate template trees.

nodes.name: manifest.py
nodes.summary: Adapts the canonical pipeline manifest into renderer-friendly GeneratorStep/GeneratorManifest models, resolving section template names, module paths, and audience labels while excluding runtime-only orchestration nodes via the shared is_orchestration_only predicate.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses frozen dataclasses for immutable step projections and raises a named MissingSectionTemplateError instead of fabricating template names (fail-fast over silent-lie pattern, per the 2026-06-12 drift-audit fold).

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: CLI and library entry point that renders the v4.2 prompt pack markdown from the pipeline manifest, workflow policy, and Jinja2 templates.

nodes.name: make_env
nodes.summary: Builds a Jinja2 Environment with a FileSystemLoader rooted at the given or bundled templates directory.

nodes.name: MissingSectionTemplateError
nodes.summary: Raised when a renderable (non-orchestration) step has no section template, naming the step and expected template path up front instead of letting Jinja crash later with TemplateNotFound.

nodes.name: GeneratorStep
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass projecting a pipeline step into the renderer-friendly shape: id, label, section anchor, template name, HUD id, module path, audience, and ordering hints.

nodes.name: GeneratorManifest
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the generator input model: schema/pack versions, generator ref, and the ordered tuple of GeneratorStep projections.

nodes.name: _template_name
nodes.summary: Resolves a step's section template by globbing sections/<id>-*.md.j2 under the template root, raising MissingSectionTemplateError with the expected slugified name when no template exists.

nodes.name: _module_path
nodes.summary: Maps step id prefixes to the implementing module path (Enrique for the ElevenLabs lane, Gary for 07-steps, the orchestrator loop for 04-steps, Desmond for 14/14.5/15, run_hud.py otherwise).

nodes.name: _orchestration_only_ids
nodes.summary: Returns the set of manifest node ids that are runtime-only orchestration (never pack prose), using the shared app.manifest.schema.is_orchestration_only predicate; legacy steps-shaped manifests yield an empty set.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports app.manifest.loader/schema lazily inside the function and swallows load failures to tolerate legacy steps-shaped manifests that carry no graph nodes.

nodes.name: load_generator_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads the canonical pipeline manifest, drops orchestration-only steps, and shapes the remaining renderable steps into a GeneratorManifest with resolved template names, module paths, and audience labels.

nodes.name: render_pack
nodes.summary: Renders the v4.2 pack markdown by loading the generator manifest and workflow policy, building the Jinja environment, rendering layout/pack.md.j2, and writing the output file with LF newlines.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point parsing --manifest and --output arguments and invoking render_pack.

nodes.name: init_postgres.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent Postgres bootstrap script that creates the 'user' role, the course_dev_ide_migration database, grants schema privileges, and enables the pgcrypto extension for the native (non-Docker) dev database.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses psql meta-commands (\gexec, \connect) and a DO block for idempotent role creation, so it can be re-run safely against an existing server.

nodes.name: fidelity_drift_check.py

nodes.name: extract_source_themes
nodes.summary: Parses extracted.md from the source bundle and returns the top-level (##) headings as themes, each with a 200-character content preview.

nodes.name: check_theme_representation
nodes.summary: Scores how strongly a source theme heading is represented in an artifact text by computing the fraction of the heading's significant words found in the artifact.

nodes.name: compute_global_drift
nodes.summary: Computes the global fidelity drift verdict for a gate by checking every source theme's representation in the artifact text against gate- and mode-specific thresholds, returning coverage ratios and the list of missing themes.

nodes.name: pack.md.j2

nodes.languageNotes: Sections are included dynamically ({% include step.template_name %}) driven by a steps manifest, so the layout itself stays section-agnostic.

nodes.name: audience_tag.j2

nodes.name: pack_header.md.j2

nodes.name: 08B-storyboard-b-hil-review.md.j2

nodes.name: 10-fidelity-quality-pre-spend.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack section template running the fidelity/quality pre-spend check, validating the locked package before any paid generation credits are consumed.

nodes.name: 11-elevenlabs-voice-selection-hil.md.j2

nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack section template for operator review of the assembled ElevenLabs input package before narration synthesis is dispatched.

nodes.name: heartbeat_check.mjs

nodes.name: loadEnv
nodes.summary: Manually parses the project-root .env file into process.env, exiting with an error if the file is missing; existing environment variables are not overwritten.

nodes.name: check

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for Marcus production-readiness capabilities (PR-PF, PR-RC, PR-HC, PR-RS), re-exporting CAPABILITY_REGISTRY and UnknownCapability from the registry module.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Builds the Marcus capability registry by parsing YAML frontmatter from sanctum capability markdown files, with a lookup API and a cross-reference audit against the supplementary registry.yaml index and schema files.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown frontmatter is the authoritative source of capability definitions; registry.yaml is only a fast programmatic index that the audit cross-checks (AC-T.9).

nodes.name: UnknownCapabilityError
nodes.summary: Router-level KeyError subclass raised when a capability code is not registered (AC-T.8), distinct from capability-execution errors carried in the ReturnEnvelope; aliased as UnknownCapability for back-compat.

nodes.name: RegistryEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding one capability's registration data: code, name, description, script_module, schema_path, full-or-stub status, and the source markdown path.

nodes.name: _parse_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Extracts and YAML-parses the frontmatter block from a capability markdown file, returning None when the file or frontmatter is absent or malformed.

nodes.name: _scan_capabilities_dir
nodes.summary: Scans the sanctum capabilities directory for markdown files with valid code/name frontmatter and builds RegistryEntry objects keyed by capability code.

nodes.name: _load_registry_yaml
nodes.summary: Loads the supplementary registry.yaml index, returning the capabilities mapping or an empty dict when the file is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: get
nodes.summary: Public lookup that returns the RegistryEntry for a capability code, raising UnknownCapability with the sorted list of known codes when the code is unregistered.

nodes.name: cross_reference_audit
nodes.summary: Audits consistency between registry.yaml, the frontmatter-derived registry, and per-capability schema files, returning a list of finding strings for any missing or mismatched entries (AC-T.9).

nodes.name: run_prompt12_narration.py

nodes.name: _load_operations_module
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports skills/elevenlabs-audio/scripts/elevenlabs_operations.py from its file path using importlib, returning the loaded module object.

nodes.name: main

nodes.summary: Cora dev-lane package barrel that re-exports the block-mode guard node, graph manifest types, and compile/load helpers used by the LangGraph dev pipeline.

nodes.name: block_mode_node.py
nodes.summary: Cora in-graph block-mode guard that dynamically loads the BMAD preclosure hook and raises a GateError when the pre-closure check denies story closure.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib to dynamically load the preclosure hook from skills/bmad-agent-cora rather than a static import, decoupling app code from skill packaging.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph manifest loader and compiler: validates the YAML dev-graph schema with strict Pydantic models, resolves dotted handler refs, and assembles a LangGraph StateGraph keyed by StoryState.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pydantic v2 strict mode (extra='forbid', validate_assignment, strict) with field/model validators to enforce dev-lane-only thread namespaces and topology integrity.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Cora handler package init exposing the shared append_checkpoint helper that builds a NodeCheckpoint with SanctumFingerprint and merges it into StoryState.

nodes.name: close_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the close_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: implement_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the implement_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: plan_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the plan_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: review_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the review_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: test_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the test_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 NodeCheckpoint model capturing per-node story progress with timezone-aware timestamps, status enum, and an embedded sanctum fingerprint.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 SanctumFingerprint model recording a UUID4 snapshot id, SHA-256 content digest, and timezone-aware capture time for sanctum state snapshots.

nodes.name: story_state.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 StoryState model representing the LangGraph dev-lane story-scoped state with a list of node checkpoints and updated_at timestamp.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint_validators.py
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator enforcing the NodeCheckpoint invariant that a 'complete' status requires a completed_at timestamp and non-complete statuses must leave it unset.

nodes.name: _load_preclosure_hook
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads the preclosure hook module from the bmad-agent-cora skills directory using importlib.util, returning the PreClosureResult class and run_preclosure_check function.

nodes.name: block_mode_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the Cora pre-closure check against StoryState and raises a GateError when closure is denied; otherwise updates updated_at.

nodes.name: DevGraphNodeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing a single Cora dev-graph node with id, label, and dotted handler reference.

nodes.name: DevGraphEdgeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing one directed edge in the dev-graph manifest, aliasing 'from' to from_node to avoid the Python keyword.

nodes.name: DevGraphManifest
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic manifest for the Cora dev-lane graph that validates schema version, node/edge topology, and enforces that thread_namespace targets the dev/ lane with a {story_id} placeholder.

nodes.name: CompiledGraphHandle
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass bundling a compiled LangGraph graph, its source manifest, and a thread-namespace template with a per-story formatter method.

nodes.name: _resolve_handler
nodes.summary: Resolves a dotted 'module:attr' handler reference into a callable, raising CompileError on bad format, missing module/attribute, or non-callable target.

nodes.name: load_dev_graph_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the dev-graph YAML manifest from disk, raising ManifestValidationError for missing files, malformed YAML, or schema violations.

nodes.name: _build_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a LangGraph StateGraph[StoryState] by adding each manifest node with its resolved handler and wiring edges, defaulting to START when no explicit start edge exists.

nodes.name: compile_dev_graph
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that coerces a manifest input (path or model), builds the StateGraph, compiles it, and returns a CompiledGraphHandle.

nodes.name: append_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Builds a complete NodeCheckpoint (with SanctumFingerprint derived from a SHA-256 of node_id) and returns the StoryState patch appending it plus refreshing updated_at.

nodes.name: NodeCheckpoint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model representing one node's checkpoint with status enum, mandatory tz-aware timestamps, optional completed_at, and a model-level invariant requiring completed_at iff status is 'complete'.

nodes.name: SanctumFingerprint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model carrying a UUID4 snapshot_id, 64-char SHA-256 content_sha256, and tz-aware captured_at, with field validators enforcing UUID version and timezone-awareness.

nodes.name: StoryState
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for the LangGraph dev-lane state keyed by story_id, holding the ordered list of NodeCheckpoint entries and a tz-aware updated_at.

nodes.name: enforce_complete_requires_completed_at
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator that raises ValueError when status=='complete' is missing completed_at, or when a non-complete status carries a completed_at value.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the gate-layer package exposing error types, operator verdict schema, party-mode-as-interrupt entry, and resume-API helpers as the canonical public surface.

nodes.name: guardrails.py
nodes.summary: Import-time and AST-source guardrails that prevent scheduler/bypass modules from being loaded or referenced inside gate-layer code, enforcing the no-scheduler invariant.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ast.walk to statically scan for forbidden import patterns; runtime sys.modules check complements the static pass.

nodes.name: verdict.py
nodes.summary: Thin gate-layer re-export of OperatorVerdict/OperatorVerdictVerb that runs scheduler-import guardrails before exposing the schema.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: HTTP bridge package scaffold that re-exports the gate-verdict endpoint as the package's public entry point.

nodes.name: gate_endpoint.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI app factory and endpoint handlers for gate verdict submission, override submission, and override application — the HTTP transport surface for the gate layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses FastAPI Header-injected operator_id, HTTPException-based error mapping, and Pydantic model_validate at the edge.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel module for the learning-ledger package surfacing emitter, event-builder, and query helpers as the canonical public API.

nodes.name: emitter.py
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed learning-ledger emitter with idempotent insert semantics, schema-bootstrap helper, failure-counter metrics, and DATABASE_URL resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses psycopg connection + JSONB payload; idempotency key short-circuits duplicate inserts to the same row.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ledger-event schemas (VerdictLedgerEvent, OverrideLedgerEvent, SanctumMutationLedgerEvent) with UUID4/tz-aware validators, canonical natural-key derivation, and SHA-256 idempotency-key computation.
nodes.languageNotes: Inheritance hierarchy under _LedgerEventBase; idempotency_key derived deterministically from natural_key via hashlib.sha256.

nodes.name: queries.py
nodes.summary: Read-side helpers for the learning ledger: reject-rate-per-gate aggregation, gate inventory listing, and sanctum-mutation event hydration with Pydantic deserialization.
nodes.languageNotes: All queries accept an optional pre-opened psycopg connection to support transactional tests.

nodes.name: gate_decide.py
nodes.summary: MCP-server tool wrappers (gate_decide_tool, override_submit_tool, override_apply_tool) that forward operator verdicts and override flows through the same resume/override APIs the HTTP endpoint uses.

nodes.name: override_api.py
nodes.summary: In-process override registry and orchestration: computes cache-invalidation impact, mints confirm tokens, applies operator verdicts to RunState, and emits OverrideLedgerEvent records to the ledger.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level registries (_RUN_STATE_REGISTRY, _PENDING_WARNINGS, _CONSUMED_TOKENS) act as test-resettable in-memory state; SHA-256 confirm tokens bind warning to apply call.

nodes.name: override_warning.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ModelOverrideWarning schema carrying cache-impact estimate, affected-node set, confirm token, and validated expires_at>issued_at invariant.

nodes.name: sanctum_watcher.py
nodes.summary: Filesystem watcher (watchdog + polling fallback) that detects sanctum markdown mutations during an active trial invocation, emits SanctumMutationLedgerEvent records, and queues pending SanctumWarning entries per trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Threading-based polling thread complements watchdog observer; SHA-256 hashes normalize CRLF to LF for cross-platform stability.

nodes.name: assert_scheduler_modules_not_loaded
nodes.summary: Raises SchedulerImportError if any scheduler-prefixed module is already present in sys.modules at import time.

nodes.name: assert_no_scheduler_or_bypass_source
nodes.summary: AST-walks a source string to reject scheduler/bypass imports and unauthorized-bypass-call patterns, raising SchedulerImportError or UnauthorizedResumeBypassError.

nodes.name: gate_verdict_endpoint
nodes.summary: Validates an OperatorVerdict payload, resumes the workflow, and builds the transport response for the verdict endpoint.

nodes.name: gate_override_submit_endpoint
nodes.summary: Forwards an override submission request to submit_override and returns the resulting ModelOverrideWarning as a JSON dump.

nodes.name: gate_override_apply_endpoint
nodes.summary: Applies an override verdict via apply_override and returns the resulting OverrideEvent together with refreshed decision-card metadata.

nodes.name: create_gate_app
nodes.summary: FastAPI app factory that wires the three gate POST routes with operator-id header injection and HTTPException-based error mapping.

nodes.name: EmissionResult
nodes.summary: Frozen result record returned by emit_ledger_event capturing status (inserted/duplicate/failed), event_id, idempotency_key, and failure reason.

nodes.name: emit_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Inserts a ledger event into Postgres with idempotency-key deduplication, schema bootstrap, JSONB payload serialization, and failure-counter increments on errors.

nodes.name: ensure_ledger_schema
nodes.summary: Executes the bundled ledger schema DDL against the provided connection to lazily create the ledger table.

nodes.name: _load_existing_event_id
nodes.summary: Looks up an existing ledger event_id for a given idempotency_key to support deduplicated inserts.

nodes.name: _LedgerEventBase
nodes.summary: Abstract Pydantic base for ledger events providing UUID4/tz-aware validators, payload-extraction, and SHA-256 idempotency_key derivation from a subclass-defined natural_key.

nodes.name: VerdictLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator gate verdict with verb, card_id, verdict_id, operator_id, and transport_kind metadata.

nodes.name: OverrideLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator model-override action across the submit/apply phases with confirm_token linkage.

nodes.name: SanctumMutationLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording a detected mutation of a sanctum markdown file with before/after SHA-256 hashes and suggested invalidating commit.

nodes.name: build_verdict_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a VerdictLedgerEvent from an OperatorVerdict instance and the originating transport kind.

nodes.name: build_override_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs an OverrideLedgerEvent for either the submit or apply phase of a model-override action.

nodes.name: build_sanctum_mutation_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a SanctumMutationLedgerEvent for a detected file mutation.

nodes.name: reject_rate_per_gate
nodes.summary: Aggregates verdict events by gate_id for a trial, returning reject-vs-total counts per gate.

nodes.name: gate_inventory
nodes.summary: Returns the distinct gate_id inventory along with first/last-seen timestamps for a trial.

nodes.name: sanctum_mutations

nodes.name: gate_decide_tool

nodes.name: override_submit_tool

nodes.name: compute_cache_impact

nodes.name: _SanctumEventHandler

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml

nodes.name: directive_model.py

nodes.name: DirectiveSource

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level directive container with UUID4 run_id, tz-aware composed_at timestamp, corpus_dir, list of DirectiveSources, and schema version.

nodes.name: compute_model_digest
nodes.summary: Computes SHA-256 hex digest of a model's canonical JSON form, used to bind operator verdicts to a specific directive snapshot.

nodes.name: load_directive
nodes.summary: Reads a directive YAML file from disk and parses it through Directive.model_validate for full Pydantic re-validation.

nodes.name: display_directive

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload
nodes.summary: Pydantic model carrying a mapping of ref_id to per-field updates for directive sources, rejecting empty update blocks.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict

nodes.name: tripwire_ledger.py

nodes.languageNotes: Applies the project Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: validate_assignment=True, extra='forbid', TypeAdapter-based explicit coercion-path red-rejection on the closed Literal alias, and field validators that delegate tz/UUID4 enforcement to app.models.state._base helpers.

nodes.name: verify_audit_chain

nodes.name: AuditChainOrderError
nodes.summary: Raised when a TripwireLedgerEntry breaks monotonic fired_at ordering for its tripwire_id in the ledger.

nodes.name: AuditChainParentLinkError
nodes.summary: Raised when a fired or marginal-fired verdict row is missing the parent trace_id required for cross-trial provenance.

nodes.name: TripwireId
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of the six Slab 7c tripwire identifiers (TW-7c-1 through TW-7c-6) used as a typed key in the ledger schema.

nodes.name: TripwireSeverity
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of severity tiers (critical / high / medium) attached to each TripwireLedgerEntry.

nodes.name: TripwireLedgerEntry

nodes.languageNotes: ConfigDict pins validate_assignment=True and extra='forbid'; pre-mode validator on tripwire_id implements the explicit triple-layer red-rejection idiom from the Pydantic-v2 schema checklist.

nodes.name: ReturnEnvelope

nodes.name: stub_envelope
nodes.summary: Constructs a canonical NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED ReturnEnvelope used by capability stubs (PR-HC, PR-RS) prior to full implementation.

nodes.summary: Invokes the readiness subprocess with a bounded timeout and captures stdout/stderr/returncode for downstream envelope assembly.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that dispatches PR-PF summarize/execute through the shared run_cli harness.

nodes.name: execute

nodes.name: first_clone_bootstrap.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows/PowerShell one-command bootstrap that verifies Python+git, creates .venv, installs editable deps with dev extras, stubs .env, installs pre-commit hooks, pings Postgres, and runs the trial-run preflight to emit a GREEN/YELLOW/RED verdict.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses [CmdletBinding()] with switch params (-SkipPostgres, -SkipPreCommit) and a here-string passed to python -c for the Postgres reachability check.

nodes.name: first_clone_bootstrap.sh
nodes.summary: Linux/macOS counterpart of first_clone_bootstrap.ps1 that performs the same 7-step fresh-clone bootstrap (Python+git check, .venv, pip install, .env stub, pre-commit, optional Postgres ping, preflight) and exits with the preflight verdict.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail with --skip-postgres / --skip-pre-commit arg parsing and a python heredoc for the Postgres ping.

nodes.name: ready_for_trial.ps1
nodes.summary: Pre-flight harness for first real trial readiness on Windows; runs 9 sequential checks (preflight, migration health dashboard, m5 pre-vote audit, cascade_config validate, model-id guard pytest suite, required-key presence, ruff, lint-imports, migration pytest) and emits READY FOR TRIAL or BLOCKED.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines an Invoke-Step helper that captures exit codes from native commands and reports per-step PASS/FAIL plus a failing-step summary at the end.

nodes.name: ready_for_trial.sh
nodes.summary: Linux/macOS counterpart of ready_for_trial.ps1; runs the same 9 trial-readiness checks via a run_step helper and prints READY FOR TRIAL or BLOCKED depending on whether any step failed.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -uo pipefail (intentionally not -e so failing steps don't abort the harness) and a bash heredoc embedded in run_required_key_check to assert OPENAI_API_KEY / LANGSMITH_API_KEY presence.

nodes.name: Invoke-Step
nodes.summary: Helper that runs a numbered preflight step (scriptblock), captures stdout/stderr, normalizes the exit code, records the result in the shared $results list, and prints PASS or FAIL with color coding.

nodes.name: run_required_key_check
nodes.summary: Invokes the venv python with an inline heredoc that loads .env via scripts.utilities.env_loader and asserts that OPENAI_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_API_KEY are set in the environment.

nodes.name: run_step
nodes.summary: Shell counterpart of Invoke-Step: runs the given command with arguments, records the exit code into STEP_RESULTS, remembers the first failed step in FAILED_STEP, and prints PASS or FAIL.
