nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marcus-Intake package entry point (steps 01-04 plus 4A pre-packet construction) from the Story 30-1 duality split; defines the PrePacketSnapshot model and routes pre-packet emission through the orchestrator single-writer write API.

nodes.name: PrePacketSnapshot
nodes.summary: Pydantic snapshot model carrying run_id, source_ref, operator_prompt, and normalized_payload for the Intake-to-Orchestrator pre-packet handoff.

nodes.name: extract_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Builds a PrePacketSnapshot from intake state and appends a pre_packet_snapshot event via the orchestrator write API, keeping Intake off the raw log surface.

nodes.name: pre_packet.py
nodes.summary: Builds the Irene handoff packet (irene-packet.md) from intake bundle artifacts and emits the typed pre_packet_snapshot event through a caller-injected dispatch seam (Stories 30-2a/30-2b); the 30-2a builder is locked byte-identical.

nodes.name: prepare_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Pure file-I/O builder that reads extracted.md, metadata.json, and operator-directives.md from the bundle directory and writes the composed irene-packet.md (LOCKED at Story 30-2a).

nodes.name: _to_repo_relative_posix
nodes.summary: Resolves a path against the repo root and returns its POSIX-style repo-relative form, rejecting paths that escape the repository.

nodes.name: _build_sme_refs
nodes.summary: Constructs SourceRef entries for the SME bundle from metadata source listings, falling back to a digest of extracted.md when explicit sources are absent.

nodes.name: prepare_and_emit_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Wraps prepare_irene_packet, computes sha256 digests of the bundle artifacts, assembles a PrePacketSnapshotPayload inside an EventEnvelope, and emits it through the injected dispatch callable (Story 30-2b).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel re-exporting the Lesson Plan public surface — schema models, append-only log, digest helpers, event primitives, registries, ModalityProducer ABC, Gagne diagnostician, fit report, and Quinn-R gate (Stories 31-1/31-2/31-3).
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel: pure re-export __init__ aggregating twelve sibling modules into one import surface.

nodes.name: blueprint_coauthor.py
nodes.summary: Irene blueprint co-author protocol (Story 29-3): validates a concrete 31-4 blueprint draft asset, records Irene plus writer approval in a deterministic sign-off sidecar markdown, and returns a PlanUnit carrying a typed BlueprintSignoff pointer.

nodes.name: _validate_blueprint_plan_unit
nodes.summary: Guards that the plan unit is in the correct scope and modality state to accept a blueprint sign-off, raising BlueprintCoauthorError otherwise.

nodes.name: _load_blueprint_markdown
nodes.summary: Resolves and reads the blueprint draft markdown referenced by the produced asset, enforcing repo-relative POSIX paths and existence before sign-off.

nodes.name: compose_blueprint_signoff_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the deterministic sign-off sidecar markdown embedding plan-unit identity, blueprint reference, and both co-author commentaries with a timezone-aware timestamp.

nodes.name: coauthor_blueprint
nodes.summary: Top-level co-author entry: validates commentaries and plan unit, writes the sign-off artifact, and returns the PlanUnit copy updated with a BlueprintSignoff pointer.

nodes.name: blueprint_producer.py
nodes.summary: First concrete ModalityProducer implementation: renders a deterministic markdown blueprint draft for plan units targeting the blueprint modality, with scope validation and an explicit human-review checkpoint.

nodes.name: _validate_blueprint_target
nodes.summary: Raises BlueprintScopeError unless the plan unit is gold-scoped, blueprint-modality, and otherwise eligible for blueprint production.

nodes.name: render_blueprint_body
nodes.summary: Default body renderer producing the deterministic markdown skeleton of the blueprint draft from the plan unit and production context.

nodes.name: compose_blueprint_markdown
nodes.summary: Wraps a rendered body with the canonical blueprint header/footer sections, including the human-review checkpoint block.

nodes.name: BlueprintProducer
nodes.summary: Concrete ModalityProducer subclass that validates the target unit, renders the blueprint markdown, writes it under the resolved output root, and returns a ProducedAsset.

nodes.name: component_type_registry.py
nodes.summary: Closed-set component-type registry (N=2 at MVP, Story 31-3): catalog of composite packages built from atomic modalities, validating at import time that every referenced modality is registered; widening requires a schema-version bump.

nodes.name: ComponentTypeEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen Pydantic entry describing a composite component type: ref, constituent modality_refs (each validated against the modality registry), description, and prompt-pack version.

nodes.name: get_component_type_entry
nodes.summary: Lookup accessor returning the registered ComponentTypeEntry for a component_type_ref, or None when unregistered.

nodes.name: coverage_manifest.py
nodes.summary: Coverage-manifest verifier (Story 32-2) auditing that every downstream plan-ref surface in the 05-to-13 path preserves lesson_plan_revision plus lesson_plan_digest and gates live consumer entrypoints with assert_plan_fresh, verified via AST inspection of consumer modules.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python ast module to statically prove that consumer entrypoints make a live, canonical assert_plan_fresh call rather than a dead or aliased one.

nodes.name: CoverageSurface
nodes.summary: Pydantic record of one audited surface: step id, owning story, module path, plan-ref mode, field presence flags, freshness-gate verification, and status.

nodes.name: CoverageSummary
nodes.summary: Aggregate counters over all surfaces (implemented/pending/deferred, plan-ref field coverage, freshness-gate gaps) plus the derived trial_ready flag.

nodes.name: CoverageManifest
nodes.summary: Top-level manifest model with schema version, timezone-aware generated_at, source story key, sorted surfaces, and summary.

nodes.name: CoverageInventoryEntry
nodes.summary: Declarative inventory row describing a surface to audit: module path, plan-ref mode, freshness requirement, consumer entrypoint, sample factory, and deferred flag.

nodes.name: verify_plan_ref_fields
nodes.summary: Checks that a sample surface payload carries lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest in the mode-appropriate location (top-level or nested plan_ref).

nodes.name: _wrapper_is_live_and_canonical
nodes.summary: AST check that a wrapper function both forwards to the canonical assert_plan_fresh call and is itself invoked on the live path, rejecting dead wrappers.

nodes.name: verify_assert_plan_fresh_usage
nodes.summary: Parses a consumer module with ast and proves the named entrypoint makes a live, canonical assert_plan_fresh call (directly or through a live wrapper) with the parsed envelope as first argument.

nodes.name: summarize_surfaces
nodes.summary: Folds resolved surfaces into a CoverageSummary, deriving the trial_ready verdict from field coverage and freshness-gate verification.

nodes.name: _resolve_status
nodes.summary: Resolves an inventory entry to implemented/pending/deferred by combining story statuses from sprint-status.yaml with module-file existence, raising on inconsistency.

nodes.name: build_coverage_manifest
nodes.summary: Drives the full audit: resolves each inventory entry, runs sample-payload field checks and AST freshness verification for implemented surfaces, and assembles the CoverageManifest.

nodes.name: emit_coverage_manifest
nodes.summary: Builds the manifest and writes its canonical JSON rendering to the target path, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: digest.py
nodes.summary: Canonical-JSON sha256 digest helpers for LessonPlan integrity (Story 31-1): None-stripping canonicalization plus compute_digest and assert_digest_matches API; the digest field is cleared before hashing.

nodes.name: _strip_none
nodes.summary: Recursively removes None-valued mapping entries from the serialized payload so optional-absent fields cannot perturb the canonical form.

nodes.name: compute_digest
nodes.summary: Dumps the LessonPlan with its digest field cleared, canonicalizes to sorted-key compact JSON, and returns the sha256 hex digest.

nodes.name: assert_digest_matches
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError when a plan's stored digest disagrees with the freshly computed canonical digest (AC-B.10).

nodes.name: event_type_registry.py
nodes.summary: Event-type registry and open-string validator (Story 31-1 AC-B.8): the nine Gagne-9 plan-unit event labels plus reserved log event types, accepting well-formed unknown ids with a once-per-type warning.

nodes.name: validate_event_type
nodes.summary: Open-set validator: enforces string type and id regex, passes known/reserved types silently, and logs a single warning per unknown event type.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Event primitives for the Lesson Plan log (Story 31-1): the generic EventEnvelope every 31-2 log event conforms to, the first concrete ScopeDecisionTransition payload, and actor-to-internal-actor normalization.

nodes.name: EventEnvelope
nodes.summary: Generic log-event shape: UUID4 event_id, timezone-aware timestamp, plan_revision, registry-validated event_type, and free-form payload.

nodes.name: ScopeDecisionTransition
nodes.summary: First concrete event payload pinning a scope-decision state transition (from/to state and scope, actor plus internal_actor, rationale snapshot) with an audit-dump serializer.

nodes.name: to_internal_actor
nodes.summary: Maps the operator-facing actor label (plus optional hint) onto the closed internal-actor vocabulary used for single-writer enforcement.

nodes.name: fit_report.py
nodes.summary: Fit-report-v1 validator, canonical serializer, and emission wiring (Story 29-1); Marcus-Orchestrator is the sole sanctioned caller of emit_fit_report — Irene produces FitReport instances but never emits them directly.

nodes.name: validate_fit_report
nodes.summary: Validates a FitReport against the live lesson plan: revision freshness (StaleFitReportError), unit-id membership (UnknownUnitIdError), and per-diagnosis consistency rules.

nodes.name: serialize_fit_report
nodes.summary: Serializes a FitReport to canonical sorted-key compact JSON aligned with the digest module's kwargs (without claiming byte-identity with compute_digest).

nodes.name: deserialize_fit_report
nodes.summary: Parses canonical JSON back into a validated FitReport model.

nodes.name: emit_fit_report
nodes.summary: Canonical Marcus-Orchestrator emission path: validates the report against the plan, wraps it in an EventEnvelope, and appends it to the LessonPlanLog under the single-writer matrix.

nodes.name: gagne_diagnostician.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic Gagne-9 diagnostician (Story 29-2): Irene-authored behavior deriving per-unit fitness, recommended scope decisions, weather bands, and formatted commentary, then aggregating a whole-plan FitReport.

nodes.name: PriorDeclinedRationale
nodes.summary: Small model carrying a previously declined unit's id and rationale so re-diagnosis can soften repeated recommendations.

nodes.name: _derive_fitness
nodes.summary: Deterministic fitness derivation for a plan unit from event-type support, modality validity, and dial settings.

nodes.name: _derive_recommended_scope_decision
nodes.summary: Maps derived fitness (plus prior-declined history) onto the recommended scope decision for the unit.

nodes.name: _format_commentary
nodes.summary: Renders Irene's deterministic commentary sentence(s) for a diagnosis, adjusting phrasing for prior-declined units and weather band.

nodes.name: diagnose_plan_unit
nodes.summary: Diagnoses a single plan unit: validates event-type support, derives fitness, scope recommendation, weather band, and commentary into a FitDiagnosis.

nodes.name: diagnose_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Diagnoses every unit in the plan (rejecting duplicate targets), assembles the FitReport with plan-ref binding, and normalizes prior-declined rationales.

nodes.name: log.py
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL Lesson Plan Log (Story 31-2): LessonPlanLog write/read API, single-writer enforcement matrix (WRITER_EVENT_MATRIX), repo-relative path validation, payload shapes, and the module-level assert_plan_fresh staleness gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-process single-writer MVP: each append writes one canonical-JSON line; writer identity is triple-surfaced via Literal schema, runtime matrix, and entry-point checks.

nodes.name: _validate_repo_relative_path
nodes.summary: Shared validator enforcing that artifact paths are repo-relative POSIX strings that resolve inside the repository.

nodes.name: SourceRef
nodes.summary: Pydantic shape for a source artifact reference: repo-relative path plus sha256, with path validation.

nodes.name: PrePacketSnapshotPayload
nodes.summary: Typed payload for pre_packet_snapshot events: run id, SME refs, artifact path, digests, and directive/ingestion flags, with repo-relative artifact-path validation.

nodes.name: PlanLockedPayload
nodes.summary: Typed payload for plan.locked events carrying the locked plan revision and its compute_digest sha256.

nodes.name: LessonPlanLog
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL log class: append_event (sole write path, enforcing the writer-event matrix), read_events with corruption detection, and latest_plan_revision/latest_plan_digest accessors.

nodes.name: assert_plan_fresh
nodes.summary: Staleness detector: compares an envelope's plan_revision/digest plan-ref against the log's latest plan.locked state and raises StalePlanRefError on mismatch.

nodes.name: modality_producer.py
nodes.summary: ModalityProducer abstract base class (Story 31-3 AC-B.3): the contract every concrete modality producer subclasses, pinning a registered modality_ref class variable and the produce(plan_unit, context) -> ProducedAsset signature.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses __init_subclass__ to validate at class-definition time that subclasses pin a registered, ready modality_ref.

nodes.name: ModalityProducer
nodes.summary: ABC enforcing via __init_subclass__ that subclasses declare a registered modality_ref, and declaring the abstract produce() contract returning a ProducedAsset.

nodes.name: modality_registry.py
nodes.summary: Closed-set modality registry (Story 31-3): catalog of five atomic producer targets at MVP (slides, blueprint, narration, research, etc.) with ready/pending status; widening requires a schema-version bump plus changelog entry.

nodes.name: ModalityEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen Pydantic registry entry: modality_ref, status (ready/pending), producer dotted-path (null iff pending, enforced by validator), and description.

nodes.name: get_modality_entry
nodes.summary: Lookup accessor returning the registered ModalityEntry for a modality_ref, or None when unregistered.

nodes.name: list_ready_modalities
nodes.summary: Returns the refs of all registry entries whose status is ready.

nodes.name: list_pending_modalities
nodes.summary: Returns the refs of all registry entries whose status is pending.

nodes.name: produced_asset.py
nodes.summary: ProductionContext and ProducedAsset Pydantic payload shapes (Story 31-3): the twin input/output contract for ModalityProducer, also consumed by fanout events and the Quinn-R two-branch gate.

nodes.name: ProductionContext
nodes.summary: Minimal input shape passed to produce(): run/plan identifiers and output root, designed as the W-2 extensibility seam for producer-specific subclassing.

nodes.name: ProducedAsset
nodes.summary: Output contract for a produced artifact: asset path, modality/event-type refs, timezone-aware produced_at, and a fulfills field regex-validated to match the source plan-unit id.

nodes.name: quinn_r_gate.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic Quinn-R step-13 two-branch gate (Story 31-5): evaluates the locked lesson plan and produced assets without mutation, yielding per-unit verdicts and a machine-readable artifact for step-13 auditing.

nodes.name: QuinnRUnitVerdict
nodes.summary: Per-unit gate verdict model: unit id, branch taken, pass/fail outcome, and rationale.

nodes.name: QuinnRTwoBranchResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate gate result with plan-ref binding, timezone-aware evaluated_at, and the full verdict list.

nodes.name: evaluate_quinn_r_two_branch_gate
nodes.summary: Core gate evaluation: groups produced assets by unit, applies the ratified-vs-declined two-branch rules per plan unit, and returns the aggregate QuinnRTwoBranchResult without mutating the plan.

nodes.name: extract_prior_declined_rationales
nodes.summary: Convenience extractor turning a gate result's declined verdicts into PriorDeclinedRationale inputs for re-diagnosis.

nodes.name: emit_quinn_r_gate_artifact
nodes.summary: Writes the gate result's canonical JSON rendering to the resolved output root for step-13 auditing.

nodes.name: retrieval_narration_grammar.py
nodes.summary: Marcus-side narration grammar for Tracy-backed research turns: converts a raw retrieval result mapping into the single operator-facing sentence Maya hears, normalizing posture and corroboration phrasing.

nodes.name: _corroborate_phrase
nodes.summary: Builds the corroboration clause of the narration sentence from the retrieval result's source count and posture.

nodes.name: render_retrieval_narration
nodes.summary: Validates the Tracy result mapping and renders the one-sentence Marcus narration inviting the operator's next move.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Lesson Plan schema primitives (Story 31-1): the Pydantic v2 contract models all downstream stories read and write — LessonPlan, PlanUnit, ScopeDecision state machine, Dials, BlueprintSignoff, FitDiagnosis/FitReport — with revision and digest discipline.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 with validate_assignment, timezone-aware datetime validators, and triple-layer red-rejection on closed enums per the project schema checklist.

nodes.name: LearningModel
nodes.summary: Small frozen model naming the learning model applied to a unit.

nodes.name: PlanRef
nodes.summary: Plan-reference pair (lesson_plan_revision + lesson_plan_digest) used to bind downstream artifacts to a specific locked plan state.

nodes.name: Dials
nodes.summary: Per-unit tuning dials (depth, interactivity, etc.) with closed value sets.

nodes.name: IdentifiedGap
nodes.summary: Model describing a content gap identified for a unit, dispatched downstream at step 07.

nodes.name: ScopeDecision
nodes.summary: Scope-decision state machine: locked-at awareness guard, Maya-consent guard, audit-dump serializer, and the canonical transition_to method enforcing legal state transitions.

nodes.name: BlueprintSignoff
nodes.summary: Typed pointer to a blueprint sign-off sidecar artifact with repo-relative path validation and timezone-aware signed_at.

nodes.name: PlanUnit
nodes.summary: Single lesson-plan unit: Gagne event type, modality, dials, scope decision, gaps, and blueprint sign-off, with red-rejection and scope/state consistency validators.

nodes.name: LessonPlan
nodes.summary: Root aggregate of plan units with revision counter and canonical digest; bump_revision/apply_revision recompute the digest via the digest module on every change.

nodes.name: FitDiagnosis
nodes.summary: Per-unit diagnostician output: fitness, recommended scope decision (red rejected), weather band, and commentary.

nodes.name: FitReport
nodes.summary: Aggregate fit report binding diagnoses to a plan revision with timezone-aware generated_at.

nodes.name: step_05_pre_packet_handoff.py
nodes.summary: Step 05 pre-packet handoff consumer boundary (Story 30-4): typed fanout envelope plus a consume entrypoint that parses the payload and gates it with the canonical assert_plan_fresh staleness check.

nodes.name: Step05PrePacketEnvelope
nodes.summary: Typed envelope for step 05 carrying the pre-packet payload plus lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest plan-ref fields.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Live consumer entrypoint: parses the raw envelope into Step05PrePacketEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the log before handing off.

nodes.name: step_06_plan_lock_fanout.py
nodes.summary: Step 06 plan-lock fanout consumer boundary (Story 30-4): typed fanout envelope plus a consume entrypoint that parses the payload and gates it with the canonical assert_plan_fresh staleness check.

nodes.name: Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Typed envelope for step 06 carrying the plan-lock payload plus lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest plan-ref fields.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Live consumer entrypoint: parses the raw envelope into Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the log before handing off.

nodes.name: step_07_gap_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Step 07 gap-dispatch consumer boundary (Story 30-4): typed envelope for identified-gap dispatch plus a consume entrypoint gated with the canonical assert_plan_fresh staleness check.

nodes.name: Step07GapDispatchEnvelope
nodes.summary: Typed envelope for step 07 carrying gap-dispatch details plus lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest plan-ref fields.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Live consumer entrypoint: parses the raw envelope into Step07GapDispatchEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the log before handing off.

nodes.name: dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator-side dispatch seam: dispatch_intake_pre_packet is the sole emitter of Intake-built pre_packet_snapshot envelopes, and dispatch_orchestrator_event is the shared orchestrator-identity dispatcher into the Lesson Plan log.

nodes.name: dispatch_intake_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Receives the envelope Intake built (but did not emit) and appends it to the Lesson Plan log under the orchestrator writer identity, preserving the single-writer invariant.

nodes.name: dispatch_orchestrator_event
nodes.summary: Shared dispatch helper: sole non-write_api caller of LessonPlanLog.append_event for orchestrator-identity events, propagating writer-matrix errors to callers.

nodes.name: fanout.py
nodes.summary: Plan-lock fanout emitter seam (Story 30-4): builds step 05+ envelopes (including per-unit gap dispatches) from a locked lesson plan and emits fanout.envelope.emitted log events through a caller-injected dispatch callable.

nodes.name: StepFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Typed fanout envelope pairing a target step id with its payload and plan-ref fields.

nodes.name: PlanLockFanoutResult
nodes.summary: Result model listing the envelopes emitted by a plan-lock fanout pass.

nodes.name: _plan_dict_for_bridge
nodes.summary: Serializes the locked plan into the bridge dict shape downstream step envelopes embed, recomputing the digest when absent.

nodes.name: _gap_envelopes_for_unit
nodes.summary: Builds one step-07 gap-dispatch envelope per identified gap on a plan unit, carrying plan-ref fields.

nodes.name: emit_plan_lock_fanout
nodes.summary: Top-level fanout: composes step 05/06/07 envelopes from the locked plan and emits a fanout.envelope.emitted event for each through the injected dispatch callable (single-writer discipline).

nodes.name: hil_intake.py
nodes.summary: Marcus HIL intake callable (Story 32-5): production bridge turning the operator's pre-collected Step 04A scope-ratification decisions into the intake callable consumed by FourALoop.run_4a, plus a confirmation-gated lesson-plan-log reset helper.

nodes.name: MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable
nodes.summary: Callable object serving pre-collected per-unit scope decisions to FourALoop.run_4a, raising MissingIntakeDecisionError when a unit has no recorded decision.

nodes.name: build_hil_intake_callable
nodes.summary: Factory validating and wrapping the operator's decision mapping into a MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable for Facade.run_4a wiring.

nodes.name: reset_lesson_plan_log
nodes.summary: Deletes the lesson-plan log file for a fresh trial run, refusing to act unless the operator confirmation flag is explicitly set (LogResetNotConfirmedError).

nodes.name: loop.py
nodes.summary: The 4A conversation loop (Story 30-3a): per-unit scope-decision intake, dial tuning, synchronous Irene reassessment via the Gagne diagnostician, and the plan-lock trigger — orchestrated by FourALoop and surfaced to Maya only through Facade.run_4a.

nodes.name: PlanAlreadyLockedError
nodes.summary: Raised when 4A work is attempted on an already-locked plan; recovery is a fresh run_4a call with a new packet.

nodes.name: FourAState
nodes.summary: Mutable loop state tracking the working plan, per-unit decisions, and iteration bookkeeping, with ratified_units/pending_units accessors.

nodes.name: intake_scope_decision
nodes.summary: Records one operator scope decision for a plan unit via ScopeDecision.transition_to and emits the scope_decision.set event through the dispatch seam.

nodes.name: tune_unit_dials
nodes.summary: Applies operator dial adjustments to a unit, bumping the plan revision and recomputing the digest before continuing the loop.

nodes.name: _voice_message_for_iteration
nodes.summary: Composes the Marcus voice line for a loop iteration, appending the Tracy retrieval narration sentence when research support is present.

nodes.name: sync_reassess_with_irene
nodes.summary: Synchronous Irene reassessment: refreshes the plan digest, runs diagnose_lesson_plan with prior-declined rationales, and folds the resulting FitReport back into loop state.

nodes.name: trigger_plan_lock_if_ready
nodes.summary: When every unit holds a terminal scope decision, recomputes the digest, locks the plan, and emits plan.locked through the dispatch seam.

nodes.name: FourALoop
nodes.summary: Per-session loop driver: run_4a iterates plan units through intake, tuning, and reassessment until the plan locks, emitting plan_unit.created events along the way.

nodes.name: maya_walkthrough.py
nodes.summary: Maya journey walkthrough (Story 32-4): drives the landed MVP chain through a canned 7-page SME fixture and records six-stage pantomime evidence — paste source, weather ribbon, gray-card click, Marcus delegation, operator rationale, card turns gold.

nodes.name: WeatherRibbonEvidence
nodes.summary: Stage-2 evidence model capturing the weather-band ribbon shown to Maya, rejecting red bands and unknown values.

nodes.name: MayaWalkthroughResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate walkthrough result bundling all six stage evidences with timezone-aware timestamps.

nodes.name: _build_canned_plan
nodes.summary: Constructs the canned LessonPlan fixture used to drive the walkthrough deterministically.

nodes.name: _canned_intake_callable
nodes.summary: Returns a deterministic intake callable serving scripted scope decisions for the canned plan units.

nodes.name: _stage_1_paste_source
nodes.summary: Stage 1: runs prepare_and_emit_irene_packet against the canned SME bundle via the real dispatch seam and records paste-source evidence.

nodes.name: _stage_6_card_turned_gold
nodes.summary: Stage 6: verifies the ratified unit's card turned gold (scope decision recorded and plan locked) and records the evidence.

nodes.name: run_maya_walkthrough

tour.title: Project Orientation: Skills Index and Environment
tour.description: Start at the project's outward-facing surface. The skills/ index advertises the BMAD agent ecosystem that wraps the runtime, while .env.example enumerates every secret the hybrid stack expects — OpenAI, LangSmith, Postgres, ElevenLabs, Gamma, Notion, and more. Reading these together answers the first newcomer question: 'what does this project do, and what does it need to run?' The project is collaborative-intelligence infrastructure for course-content production — a Marcus orchestrator plus a roster of specialists, HIL gates with DecisionCard verdicts, a tamper-evident ledger, and a reproducibility ceremony, all on LangChain/LangGraph. As of mid-2026 the pipeline completes full production runs: corpus in, composition-ready lesson bundle out.

tour.title: Operator Entry Point: The Marcus CLI
tour.description: Every production trial begins at `python -m app.marcus.cli trial start`. The `__main__.py` dispatcher wires the top-level subcommands — `trial`, `adhoc`, and gate operations — into a single argparse parser. `trial.py` implements the full lifecycle: G0 directive confirmation in the operator's editor, `run_production_trial`, resume-on-verdict, and the recover path that restarts a trial paused on a specialist dispatch error. `gate_cli.py` is the bridge operators use to submit gate verdicts and override payloads from JSON. This is the surface the README's quick-start command terminates at, and the BFS root of the whole dependency graph.
tour.languageLesson: Python's `__main__.py` makes a package directly runnable with `python -m <package>`. It's the idiomatic way to package a CLI without shipping a separate console-script entry — the package itself is the executable.

tour.title: Production Runner: The Trial Engine
tour.description: Once the CLI hands off, control lands in the production orchestrator — the highest fan-out code module in the codebase. `production_runner.py` walks the compiled manifest graph end to end: G0 directive confirm, corpus intake, lesson planning, slide and storyboard generation, narration, audio, QA, and finally the compositor assembly hand-off — pausing at conversational gates with pre-gate-Marcus drafted decision cards and supporting both resume-from-verdict and recover-from-error-pause. `dispatch_adapter.py` is its composition bridge: it builds per-specialist scaffold input state from the dispatch registry, invokes the specialist's LangGraph, and extracts contributions back into the shared envelope. `gate_runner.py` adds the shared guardrails (override-rate tripwires, Marcus/payload duality assertions), and `conversation_persistence.py` chains every gate turn with SHA-256 digests for tamper evidence.

tour.title: The Pipeline Manifest: Declarative Graph Assembly
tour.description: The production runner doesn't hardcode the flow it walks — it compiles it from a YAML pipeline manifest. `schema.py` defines the Pydantic v2 `PipelineManifest`, node and edge shapes with cross-field validators, including the `dependency_projections` that declare exactly which envelope keys flow across each edge — the architectural spine of the data-plane grounding regime. `loader.py` parses the YAML; `compiler.py` resolves dotted handler references and assembles the LangGraph `StateGraph`. This declarative layer is what makes the multi-agent flow reconfigurable: change the manifest, get a different graph. CLAUDE.md's 'pipeline lockstep regime' guards exactly these files.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 with `model_config = ConfigDict(validate_assignment=True)` rejects invalid mutations after construction, not just at creation. Combined with `Field` constraints and `model_validator`, it gives YAML configs the same red-rejection discipline as code-level types.

tour.title: Production Envelopes: The Trial's Data Spine
tour.description: Everything the runner from Step 3 produces flows into one append-only structure. `production_envelope.py` defines `SpecialistContribution` — a single specialist output carrying cost, model, provenance, and a canonical SHA-256 output digest — and `ProductionEnvelope`, the per-trial collection with UUID4 and timezone enforcement. `production_trial_envelope.py` wraps it in the full lifecycle model: preset, corpus path, paused-at-gate and paused-at-error status fields, LangSmith trace id, and invariant checks that flag inconsistent states. `trial_economics_report.py` rolls contributions up into per-agent cost entries, drift alerts against a rolling median, and budget status — the substrate of the trial economics audit.

tour.title: The Specialist Scaffold: 9-Node Pattern
tour.description: Every specialist is built from the same 9-node LangGraph template: receive → plan → act → verify → reflect → emit_spans → gate → finalize → handoff. `_scaffold/contract.py` defines the conformance contract and node-set (fan-in 20 — every specialist depends on it); `_scaffold/graph.py` is the reference builder that asserts reachability and installs the interrupt at the gate node. This uniformity is what lets the dispatch adapter from Step 3 invoke any specialist by id without special-casing — the gate interrupt is where HIL DecisionCards surface. The next four steps follow the specialists in pipeline order, from corpus to composition.

tour.title: From Corpus to Lesson Plan
tour.description: The pipeline's first act turns raw course material into a ratified lesson plan. Texas's `retrieval_dispatch.py` resolves the directive's corpus directory and produces the source bundle; `source_bundle.py` is the shared fail-loud reader that resolves a `bundle_reference` to extracted corpus text — if source content is missing it raises a `SourceBundleError` so the trial error-pauses instead of letting planner LLMs confabulate. Irene Pass-1's act body then assembles the planning prompt from sanctum digest, references, and that corpus text, parsing the LLM response into a lesson plan with per-plan-unit operator ratification. `lesson_plan/schema.py` (fan-in 16) is the Pydantic contract everything downstream reads: LessonPlan, PlanUnit, the ScopeDecision state machine, Dials, and digest discipline.

tour.title: The Data-Plane Grounding Regime
tour.description: Four mechanisms keep specialist hand-offs grounded in real upstream data — the fix for the Trial-3 vocabulary fork where placeholder slides were silently approved. `dispatch_errors.py` (fan-in 14) is the shared base for typed dispatch errors: the runner catches `SpecialistDispatchError` to convert transient external-API failures into recoverable error-pauses instead of crashed trials. Payload contracts (`CONSUMED_PAYLOAD_KEYS` in each specialist's `payload_contract.py`) freeze the vocabulary of envelope keys each act body reads, so the Ratchet-D manifest lockstep test can reject `dependency_projections` that use undeclared keys. `package_builders.py` closes the loop with pure no-LLM builder nodes that project upstream contributions into exactly that consumed vocabulary before dispatch. And `narration_join.py` is the single shared home of the narration join policy, so the Storyboard-B publisher, Enrique's audio synthesis, and Quinn-R's G5 QA all validate the identical join.
tour.languageLesson: A shared exception base class (`SpecialistDispatchError`) turns error handling into a contract: the runner catches one type, while each seam subclasses it with provider-specific context. Combined with module-level frozen constants (`CONSUMED_PAYLOAD_KEYS`) pinned by ratchet tests, Python gives you compile-time-like contracts without a compiler.

tour.title: Slides and Storyboards: Gary to Gate 2
tour.description: With the lesson plan locked, the visual pipeline begins. Gary's `gamma_dispatch.py` is the fail-loud seam to Gamma deck generation — live call or explicit fixture receipt, tagged `GammaDispatchError` on any pre-receipt failure. The `slide_content.py` writer pins Gary's output into the slide-indexed `GarySlideContent` schema that every downstream step keys against. `storyboard_publisher.py` then publishes the ONLINE interactive storyboard at the review gates by running the storyboard generator against the envelope's Gary contribution before the decision card is issued, while `storyboard_html_emitter.py` renders the HTML preview the operator actually reviews at Gate 2 (Storyboard A) and again at Storyboard B. `per_slide_subgraph.py` fans the review out per slide via LangGraph `Send`, joins results in slide-index order, and emits an EscapeCard when revise-count oscillation crosses the threshold.

tour.title: Narration, Audio, QA, and Assembly
tour.description: The back half of the pipeline turns approved storyboards into a composition-ready bundle. Irene's `pass_2_template.py` is the strict Pydantic envelope for Pass-2 narration authoring — Gary slide outputs, perception artifacts, and segment manifests with cross-artifact shape validation. `section_09_lock.py` enforces the Gate-3 lock by loading four upstream artifacts (Gary slide-content, Kira motion-plan, Vera fidelity verdict, Quinn-R QA verdict) and cross-checking plan_unit_id consistency. Enrique's act body then synthesizes narration segments with VTT captions through the ElevenLabs seam, Quinn-R's `quality_control_dispatch.py` runs the G5 grounding and pre/post-composition validator suites, and the Compositor's `_act.py` performs the final deterministic, LLM-free assembly: copying visual and motion assets into the bundle, regenerating the assembly guide, and recording content-addressed hashes for the operator hand-off.

tour.title: Gates, Decision Cards, and HIL Verdicts
tour.description: Every pause you saw in Steps 9-10 surfaces through the gate layer — the human-in-the-loop frontier. `resume_api.py` (fan-in 25) registers decision cards with cryptographic digests, validates server-issued nonces, and emits ledger events when a verdict resumes the LangGraph run. Each gate hands the operator a typed DecisionCard: `_base.py` defines the shared digest-bearing `DecisionCardBase` with cache-state and override-trail metadata, and `g2c.py` shows a concrete card carrying readiness status and blocking issues; matching JSON Schemas are emitted from these Pydantic models so external clients can validate verdicts without importing Python. `party_mode_as_interrupt.py` is the most striking design choice: multi-agent BMAD party-mode deliberation is itself modeled as a gate interrupt, so consensus rounds resume the graph exactly like any other operator verdict. `hil_intake.py` converts operator-pre-collected intake into the orchestrator's callable contract.
tour.languageLesson: JSON Schema files emitted from Pydantic v2 (`model_json_schema()`) serve as the cross-language contract. The Python source-of-truth and the JSON artifact must stay in lockstep — a common anti-pattern is editing only one side. Triple-layer red-rejection (Field constraint + validator + closed enum) is the project's defense against schema drift.

tour.title: RunState: The Pydantic Source-of-Truth
tour.description: The domain-models layer is the project's bedrock. `_base.py` enforces timezone-aware datetimes and UUID4 version across every state model (fan-in 47 — the most depended-upon file in the codebase). `run_state.py` (fan-in 37) is the authoritative LangGraph `RunState` root: UUID4 run id, lifecycle timestamps, model-resolution trail, sanctum/marcus fingerprints, story states. `SpecialistEnvelope` carries every cross-boundary payload — it is what the payload contracts from Step 8 read keys out of — and `OperatorVerdict` enforces no-tamper verbs and verb-payload consistency. If you change a shape here, you change the entire system.

tour.title: Parity Contracts and the Cora Dev-Graph
tour.description: Two adjacent substrate systems keep the runtime honest. The parity-contracts package (fan-in 20) decorates surfaces with `@parity_contract`, registering them in a typed registry so audit, sanctum, and reference-surface checks can enforce drift-free transport semantics across the HTTP, MCP, and in-process surfaces. Cora is the BMAD dev-session orchestrator implemented as its own LangGraph: `cora/graph.py` compiles a story-state machine (plan → implement → test → review → close) from a YAML dev-graph manifest, and `block_mode_node.py` is the pre-closure guard that refuses to close a story until the BMAD preclosure hook clears.

tour.title: Runtime Infrastructure: Checkpointer, Ledger, Transports
tour.description: Underneath the orchestrator sits the durability and transport layer. `checkpointer.py` wires LangGraph's AsyncPostgresSaver to `DATABASE_URL`, which is what makes gate pauses and error-pauses resumable across processes — the substrate of the trial recover path from Step 2. The learning ledger emits verdict, override, and sanctum-mutation events with idempotent inserts into the append-only `ledger_events` Postgres table (JSONB payload, unique idempotency key, composite trial/gate indexes). Two parallel transports surface the gate API: FastAPI (`http/gate_endpoint.py`) and MCP stdio (`gate_decide` tool). Both routes converge on the same resume/override APIs — exactly what the parity contracts from Step 13 enforce.
tour.languageLesson: Append-only event tables pair a UNIQUE idempotency key with INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING: retried emits become harmless no-ops instead of duplicates. Deriving the key as a SHA-256 over the event's natural identity makes idempotency deterministic rather than caller-supplied.

tour.title: BMAD Skills and the v4.2 Pack Generator
tour.description: The capstone is the ecosystem around the runtime. Each specialist has a corresponding BMAD persona SKILL.md (over 100 documents across the agent-skills layer) — Cora orchestrates dev sessions, Gary delegates to Gamma for slide generation — activated via the `_bmad/memory/bmad-agent-<name>/` sanctum trees referenced in CLAUDE.md. Parallel to the runtime, the v4.2 prompt-pack generator (`scripts/generators/v42/`) renders a complete operator-facing lesson-planning pack from the same pipeline manifest, using Jinja2 templates per section (the pack layout template alone pulls in 22 section templates mirroring the gates you toured in Steps 9-11). The runtime executes the work, the skills define the personas that author and review it, and the generated pack is the printable contract operators read during a live trial.
tour.languageLesson: Jinja2 with `StrictUndefined` raises on any unresolved variable instead of silently rendering empty — combined with a deterministic environment factory and fixed newline handling, this produces byte-stable pack outputs that hash identically across runs. Reproducibility at the template layer is what lets the 'frozen-graph reproducibility ceremony' work end-to-end.
